payload.title: Consolidate operator-loop ergonomics gaps after local evidence-pipeline commissioning
payload.summary: Recent narada-andrey operator-surface work exposed several recurring ergonomics gaps that slow architect/builder loops and increase context burn. These are adjacent to the compact workboard, compact inbox-submit, and overlay-inspect CAPAs already submitted, but this proposal groups the remaining loop-level issues.

observations.finding: No compact sanctioned architect next-loop command
observations.detail: Architect still hand-rolls workboard parsing and review/nudge checks instead of using one canonical command.
observations.impact: Role-loop behavior depends on social memory and ad hoc shell parsing.

observations.detail: Even compact Select-Object/Format-List output wraps long scalar fields like submitted_with_known_strategy across multiple lines.
observations.impact: Readable compact evidence becomes noisy and harder to scan.

observations.finding: Task creation and work-next output shapes are inconsistent for automation
observations.detail: task create echoes full JSON, while work-next may return a string packet or structured JSON depending on outcome.
observations.impact: Agents need conditional parsing and may dump more text than needed.

observations.finding: Dirty working tree ownership is not visible enough
observations.detail: After builder/architect loops, dirty files remain, but local tools do not clearly classify ownership: Bob-owned, architect-owned, stale, evidence-only, or unrelated.
observations.impact: Architect spends time manually deciding what can be committed or ignored.

observations.detail: CAPA was submitted, but the local workflow can still trigger repeated property-missing errors.
observations.impact: Until fixed, inspect commands need guardrails or a compact helper.

observations.detail: Operator says next, architect interprets duties, builder interprets next, but Narada does not yet expose this as a first-class role-loop primitive.
observations.impact: Correct behavior depends on remembered convention rather than governed machinery.

requests.text: Implement a canonical compact architect-loop command that summarizes pending reviews, architect-owned work, blocked builder tasks, underspecified handoffs, dirty ownership posture, and recommended next action.
Add output modes that are stable for agent consumption and chat display, avoiding PowerShell table/list wrapping and full payload echo by default.
Normalize work-next/task-create output shapes so success, blocked, no-work, and pulled-work states are consistently machine-readable and compact-renderable.
Add a dirty ownership classifier for Narada Sites that groups changed files by task/report/evidence/agent ownership and flags unknown ownership.

Document role-loop primitives: an operator nudge such as next should enter an intent interpretation path that resolves to architect duties, builder work processing, review closure, or blocker reporting without relying on magic-spell memory.

payload.authority_note: Operator asked to submit observed ergonomics issues after commissioning the local scale-relative evidence pipeline.
